title: armeria vulgaris var. maritima
intro: Armeria vulgaris var. maritima, also known as Sea Thrift or Maritima Thyme, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant is used in herbal medicine, primarily for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Armeria vulgaris var. maritima include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create cough syrups, salves, and ointments to support overall health and well-being.
